hero_subtitle: SFTY structures adverse media alerts into case workflows that connect screening results, regulatory rules and audit evidence for efficient compliance review.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Compliance slows when adverse media alerts accumulate
problem_description: Compliance teams face fragmented insights and manual triage when adverse media alerts come from multiple sources. This slows case review, weakens audit traceability and complicates consistent decision-making across workflows.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY aggregates adverse media alerts from multiple providers and links them to customer profiles and risk signals, creating structured cases with complete evidence and clear audit trails for compliance teams.
workflow_title: From adverse media alerts to cases
workflow_description: SFTY routes adverse media alerts through risk rules and review steps into structured cases with decisions, evidence capture and regulatory reporting workflows.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond adverse media. One evidence-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY turns fragmented adverse media alerts into structured cases with decision evidence, routing and audit readiness without replacing existing screening providers.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with secure orchestration, audit-ready evidence and deployment controls to manage compliant adverse media alert workflows and bank due diligence.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support adverse media alert workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how adverse media alerts route into case reviews
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence preserves compliance decisions
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration for multi-source alert handling
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for adverse media alerts?
faq_1_answer: SFTY structures adverse media alerts into reviewable cases, connecting results with customer, transaction and risk data for streamlined compliance workflows and consistent decision-making.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle adverse media workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes adverse media alerts into workflows that track review actions, escalate cases, log decisions and link evidence to support operational and regulatory requirements.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves detailed case histories, decision logs and evidence packages to support audit readiness and internal or external compliance reviews.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance tools?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects outputs from screening providers and compliance tools into one operational layer without replacing existing systems.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace adverse media screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above adverse media providers, organizing their alerts into structured workflows rather than replacing the underlying screening tools.
final_title: Turn adverse media alerts into structured cases
final_description: Review how SFTY connects adverse media screening, regulatory rules and evidence into controlled compliance workflows for VASPs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
